item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please don't make fake links. I personally don't consider wikidot markup to be a canon element, but even if you do, I read on mobile. They literally hurt my fingers.

Also… I felt like this had an excessive amount of specific detail and was pretty short on story. All of the exact meters and factions of seconds and extensive containment procedures just left me impatient for the other shoe to drop, but it never did.

It's good that you gave us characters to empathize with. The scout leader's story was a great exercise in making me feel empathy for someone within the format of a report, and the expansion to staff was interesting, but all in all this feels… more like a specific tone or feeling than a story. Sometimes that's all it takes, but here, for me, it wasn't enough.

Also I don't blame you if you hadn't read SCP-1008, but it is one of my favorite early SCPs, and it's also shorter than this.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1. scp-1008 did this better, and I still wasn't crazy about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, in hindsight that's a little dumb. I only included it as a sort of easter egg (235.86 seconds is equal to 3.931 minutes). I'll edit it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Initially, I thought the idea of a perpetual night syndrome was trite, but the execution of this is pristine and so I gave it a +1. There's one point that loses my immersion, which is that they were able to record all simultaneous instances of the "night terrors" to an accuracy of hundredths-of-a-second. But this is not theoretically impossible, so whatever. My favorite line is: "Notably, there were common reports of nocturnal animals sighted during the day (including badgers, owls and raccoons)."

item.title: Re: Simple Recommendation
item.content:encoded: I didn't even consider that. Fixed it. Thank you!

item.title: Simple Recommendation
item.content:encoded: This article is incredibly well made. Good job on the whole thing. Very enticing for the start to the finish with a very good concept that makes me want more. The only thing I would change is that you have the name of the park expunged but you leave the full Coordinates within the Containment procedures. A quick copy and paste with a google search and some of the mystery is gone. Maybe if you don't want to black out all the coordinates maybe expunge one axis or something, but you lose some mystery that you intend later with them being there. Anyway that's just my opinion great work, you have earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. Very interesting concept, I read it all the way through and was hooked in from the start. Fantastic job.
